findings.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
findings.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

completeness.name: completeness
completeness.summary: 100% of stops state wheelchair accessibility (49% marked accessible, 51% marked not accessible). This measures what the feed publishes, not whether a stop is physically usable. Fare data is published.

findings.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
findings.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
findings.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
findings.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

findings.effort: One setting in most export tools.
findings.fix: Remove past service periods the next time you export the feed.
findings.what: Some service calendars in the feed have already expired.
findings.why: Expired calendars are dead weight and can hide real schedule problems from your staff and vendors.

findings.effort: Usually a re-export from your scheduling software.
findings.fix: Schedule a feed re-export now so the new calendar is live well before the current one ends.
findings.what: This feed's service calendar runs out within the next 30 days.
findings.why: If a new feed isn't published before it ends, trip planners will drop your agency and riders will see no service.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'missing_feed_contact_email_and_url' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Missing feed contact email and url (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'route_long_name_contains_short_name' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Route long name contains short name (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'service_window_outside_feed_period' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Service window outside feed period (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

findings.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'unknown_file' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
findings.what: Unknown file (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
findings.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

correctness.name: correctness
correctness.summary: The MobilityData validator flagged 7 kinds of issue across 51 instances (0 error, 39 warning, 12 informational).

findings.fix: Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
findings.what: Service data runs out in 22 day(s).
findings.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency. Fixing it now is calmer than after riders notice.

freshness.name: freshness
freshness.summary: Service data runs out in 22 day(s). Publish an updated feed soon or riders will lose trip planning.

findings.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
findings.what: 1 of 7 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
findings.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

findings.effort: A vendor support ticket with the trip ids attached.
findings.fix: Ask your AVL vendor to check vehicle-to-trip assignments for the flagged trips.
findings.what: 4 of 26 sampled vehicle positions were far from their assigned route (worst: 11579 m).
findings.why: A bus shown off its route usually means a wrong trip assignment; riders watch their bus drive the wrong streets.

findings.fix: Spot-check the flagged predictions against what buses actually did; raise trip-matching with your AVL vendor.
findings.what: Some live predictions disagree with the schedule by more than 30 minutes.
findings.why: Differences that large usually mean predictions are keyed to the wrong trips, not that buses are that late.

realtime.name: realtime
realtime.summary: Sampled 9 times: 3 of 3 feeds healthy; 85.7% of scheduled trips had live predictions; 88.5% of vehicles on their route; predictions ran a median of 660s behind schedule.

criteria.detail: Service data runs out in 22 days; renew to qualify.
States wheelchair access on 100% of stops and 100% of trips.

conformance.summary: This feed is close to the conformance mark. Service data runs out in 22 days; renew to qualify.

ntd_id_alignment.detail: Setting your GTFS agency_id to your five-digit NTD ID is an optional way to line a feed up with its National Transit Database record. FTA links the two on your P-50 form, so it is not a required feed change. We don't have your NTD ID on file, so this is not checked yet.

pillars.detail: Service data runs out in 22 days; renew before you certify.

ntd_readiness.summary: This feed is close to NTD-ready. Service data runs out in 22 days; renew before you certify.

recommendations.effort: A short text field per affected stop; start with the busiest.
recommendations.fix: Add tts_stop_name with the spoken form, e.g. 'Main Street and Second Avenue', for the affected stops.
recommendations.what: 14 stop name(s) use abbreviations or symbols a screen reader may mispronounce, with no spoken form set ("Gray 30th St Station", "49th St", "9th St Lansdale", and more).
recommendations.why: Riders who use a screen reader hear the raw name, so 'Main St & 2nd Ave' can come out as 'Main saint' or drop the ampersand.

top_fixes.fix: Re-export with a validity window that reaches at least 60 days out.
top_fixes.what: Service data runs out in 22 day(s).
top_fixes.why: When the calendar runs out, trip planners stop showing this agency. Fixing it now is calmer than after riders notice.

top_fixes.effort: A few minutes per flagged stop.
top_fixes.fix: Check the flagged stops' coordinates and the route shape in your scheduling software; re-snap whichever is misplaced.
top_fixes.what: Some stops sit far from the route line they belong to.
top_fixes.why: Trip planners may draw the bus route through the wrong streets or point riders to the wrong corner.

top_fixes.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
top_fixes.what: 1 of 7 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
top_fixes.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.
